title: Build scripture figure entity model — people (Moses, Abraham, Isaiah, Nephi, Alma, Moroni, Peter, Paul, John, etc.) from LDS core canon verse text
Expand places registry with biblical and Book of Mormon geography — Jerusalem, Jordan River, Galilee, Sinai, Zarahemla, Bountiful, Cumorah, etc.
Annotate scripture verse HTML with entity spans so verse text shows person/place/thing chips in the discovery panel
Build cross-scripture entity connections — when a figure appears in multiple books/chapters, link those occurrences in the profile panel
Build things/artifacts registry for scripture objects — temple, tabernacle, altar, seer stone, iron rod, tree of life, etc.
Deepen intra-canonical semantic links — Book of Mormon verses that echo Old Testament patterns should connect more directly than external sources
Add non-Bible word-study fallbacks for Book of Mormon, D&C, Pearl of Great Price so word clicks always find a relevant scripture cross-reference
Source cleanup: fix Enuma Elish and remaining Ancient Texts that open on OCR noise rather than primary text
Wikipedia enrichment: all scripture figures and priority LDS people
description: Run build_entity_wikipedia.py to fetch Wikipedia summary, description, born/died dates, roles, and related links for all scripture figures (Moses, Nephi, etc.) and priority modern LDS figures (Holland, Maxwell, McConkie, etc.). Write enriched data to people.json.

title: Wikipedia enrichment: places and things registries
description: Run Wikipedia enrichment for all 48 places (Jerusalem, Jordan River, Sinai, etc.) and 26 things (Liahona, Urim and Thummim, Ark of the Covenant, etc.). Add wikipedia_summary, coordinates, region, historical_significance fields.

title: Cross-link graph: map person↔person, person↔place, person↔event relationships
description: For each entity, derive related_persons, related_places, related_events from Wikipedia links, scripture co-occurrence, and Donaldson corpus. Build a full cross-link graph so clicking Moses shows Aaron, Miriam, Sinai, Exodus event, Ten Commandments.

title: Corpus analytics: compute entity mention counts, most cited scriptures, most cited topics per person
description: Scan all GC talks and Donaldson files per entity: count corpus_mentions, find most_referenced_scripture (e.g. Holland → John 14:27), most_referenced_topic. This enables queries like "which apostles cite Isaiah most" or "what scripture does Maxwell reference in talks about the Atonement".

title: Topical guide: add Wikipedia concept descriptions to all 49 topics (Faith, Repentance, Atonement, etc.)
description: For each topic in topics.json, fetch its Wikipedia concept page (e.g. Faith in Christianity, Repentance, Atonement in Christianity). Add wikipedia_summary, canonical_verse (single most central scripture), and sub_topics fields. This makes each topic a knowledge node like a Wikipedia article.

title: Topical guide: rank scripture refs by semantic centrality and surface top 10 canonical verses per topic
description: The 2,595 scripture refs for Faith are alphabetically sorted. Rerank by semantic similarity score from the FAISS index. Surface the top 10 most central verses as canonical_scriptures (e.g. Faith → Hebrews 11:1, Moroni 10:4, Alma 32:21). These become the primary display in the topic profile panel.

title: Topical guide: link topics to key events and figures (Faith of Abraham, faith of Nephi, etc.)
description: For each topic, identify the key scriptural events and figures that embody it. Faith → binding of Isaac (Abraham), Nephi going to Jerusalem, Brother of Jared piercing the veil. Add related_events and key_figures_for_topic so clicking Faith shows these exemplars, not just an alphabetical list.
